channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Almazan and Fernandez families work together in the search for hidden treasure in The Secrets of Hotel 88
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios released a feature story about the continuing developments in the television series “The Secrets of Hotel 88.” The article focuses on the cooperation between the Almazan and Fernandez ...

item.title: Expansion of Pharmacy First scheme to allow pharmacists to prescribe additional medications
item.description: From this autumn, pharmacies in England will be authorised to prescribe a wider range of medications under the Pharmacy First scheme, an initiative launched in 2024 to improve access to care and reduc...

item.title: Presidency Raises Alarm Over Deepfake Videos and Religious Disinformation Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Nigerian Presidency has issued a strong warning to citizens about the rising spread of fake digital content, especially deepfake videos and manipulated audio clips being circulated online. Accordi...

item.title: Canada's Diplomatic Tone on Israel-Palestine Faces Criticism for Imbalance
item.description: Avi Benlolo critiques Prime Minister Mark Carney’s recent diplomatic engagements with Israeli President Isaac Herzog and Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas, highlighting what he sees as a t...

item.title: Opinion on Kamloops burial claims and the state of Canada’s reconciliation discourse
item.description: This opinion article by Terry Glavin examines the public and political fallout surrounding the 2021 reports of alleged unmarked graves at former residential school sites in Canada, with a particular f...

item.title: Stormers confirm key players fit ahead of URC quarter-final against Cardiff
item.description: The Stormers have finalised their team for the United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-final against Cardiff, with captain Ruhan Nel returning at outside centre alongside Dan du Plessis. This pairing ...

item.title: Culture as the Storefront: How Authenticity is Reshaping Social Commerce in Nigeria
item.description: The article explores how modern digital commerce is increasingly driven by authenticity and cultural connection rather than traditional advertising. It describes the rise of “shoppertainment,” where s...

item.title: Podcast explores why UK homes and cities are overheating amid rising extreme heat and climate change
item.description: In this episode of the Guardian’s Today in Focus podcast, environment editor Fiona Harvey examines why homes and cities across the UK are becoming increasingly hot, particularly in the context of reco...

item.title: Multiple artists withdraw from Trump-linked Freedom 250 festival amid political concerns
item.description: The Freedom 250 festival, a large-scale event tied to celebrations of the United States’ 250th anniversary, has suffered a series of high-profile withdrawals from its planned lineup, raising questions...

item.title: SIU investigates Tembisa Hospital corruption involving stolen ID and property purchases
item.description: The Special Investigations Unit (SIU) is probing a corruption case at Tembisa Hospital where a stolen ID was allegedly used to divert funds and acquire property. Oscar Nobungwana, 33, reportedly regis...

item.title: Cambridge researchers use human organoids to restore nerve regeneration
item.description: Scientists at the University of Cambridge have developed miniature lab-grown brain and spinal cord systems, called organoids, which mimic the human nervous system's communication pathways. Using these...

item.title: High Court Halts Kenya Ebola Quarantine Plans Amid Public Safety Concerns
item.description: Former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua has openly criticised the Kenyan government, accusing it of mismanagement, corruption, and putting public health at risk. His comments followed the High Court'...

item.title: President and CCM Officials Defend Police Role During 2025 Election Crisis
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan, together with CCM Secretary-General Asha Rose Migiro, publicly praised the Tanzanian police for their actions during the October–November 2025 election crisis, saying th...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada posts bail before Sandiganbayan over graft case linked to flood control projects
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada appeared before the Sandiganbayan on Friday to post a P90,000 bail in connection with a graft case involving alleged irregularities in flood control projects. The case forms pa...

item.title: APC aspirant alleges exclusion from Imo State Assembly primary after spending over N100 million
item.description: An All Progressives Congress (APC) aspirant for the Ahiazu-Mbaise Constituency seat in the Imo State House of Assembly, Ezechukwu Obinna, has publicly protested what he described as his exclusion from...

item.title: Marcos shares halo-halo recipe with Emperor Naruhito during Tokyo visit
item.description: During President Ferdinand “Bongbong” Marcos Jr.’s official visit to Tokyo, he shared a light-hearted diplomatic moment with Emperor Naruhito of Japan involving the popular Filipino dessert halo-halo....

item.title: Experts highlight fire risks associated with rooftop solar panels following Northamptonshire house blaze
item.description: Experts have issued renewed warnings about the potential fire risks linked to rooftop solar panel systems following a serious incident in Northamptonshire, where a £600,000 new-build home was engulfed...

item.title: NRL 2026 Round 13 Preview: Cronulla Sharks vs Manly Sea Eagles
item.description: In this NRL 2026 round 13 Friday night clash, the Cronulla Sharks take on the Manly Sea Eagles at what promises to be an entertaining matchup. The Sharks have experienced a dip in form recently, dropp...

item.title: Ibrahima Konaté set to depart Liverpool following collapse of contract negotiations
item.description: Ibrahima Konaté is set to leave Liverpool on a free transfer when his contract expires next month after talks over a new deal broke down. The France international had previously indicated he was close...

item.title: Rail services to and from Quetta remain suspended amid security concerns and ongoing disruptions
item.description: Train services to and from Quetta, as well as several routes across parts of Balochistan, remained suspended for another day on Friday, according to railway officials who cited unavoidable circumstanc...
